comments.author: schrockn
comments.body: i thought we were doing erk pr check instead of erk exec pr-check-complete

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed: replaced `erk exec pr-check-complete` with `erk pr check --stage=impl` in plan-implement.md. Migrated the functionality into `check_cmd.py` as a `--stage=impl` flag, removing the separate exec script.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier Review**: Use named tuple instead of bare `tuple[bool, str]` for check results. The plan mentions creating a `PrCheck` named tuple to replace this pattern throughout. This improves clarity and type safety.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Applied: added `PrCheck(NamedTuple)` with `passed: bool` and `description: str` fields to `check_cmd.py`, replacing `list[tuple[bool, str]]` throughout.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 16:22 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier Review**: Using try/except for control flow (EAFP) - prefer LBYL. Check if `impl_dir` exists or if `validate_plan_linkage` might return None instead of raising ValueError. See dignified-python-core.md: LBYL pattern.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Applied: replaced try/except around `validate_plan_linkage` with LBYL — now calls `extract_leading_issue_number` and `read_plan_ref` directly, checks for mismatch before building the error message.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 16:22 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier Review**: Conditional expression on same line as assignment for `local_config.plans_repo`. Could improve clarity by extracting to separate variable or conditional block for readability.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Applied: extracted `local_config.plans_repo` conditional to a separate if/else block with explicit `plans_repo: str | None` type annotation.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 16:22 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Python Review**: Inline import - Move `import json` to module-level imports at top of file. Only inline imports allowed for circular deps, TYPE_CHECKING, or conditional features (dignified-python-core.md)

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Removed inline `import json` from test_pr_check_stage_impl_all_checks_pass (commit a29660a72). The `json` module is already imported at module level (line 3).

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 16:33 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
